metadata.title: Debate Pressure Workspace
metadata.subtitle: Contradiction-heavy ingest fixture
metadata.description: A debate-oriented packet that stresses contradiction, dissent, and citation pressure.
watchFor: Dissent should remain close enough to inspect without flattening the main claim.
Low-confidence contradiction nodes should feel softer than the high-confidence scaffold.
The plain and field renderers should tell the same layout story.

guideSteps.label: Primary dissent
guideSteps.description: A softer contradiction that should stay nearby but not dominate.

guideSteps.label: Evidence spine
guideSteps.description: The supporting evidence chain anchoring the claim.

tasks.title: Trace support for the claim
tasks.prompt: Follow the consensus claim through its evidence and citation spine.

tasks.title: Find the weakest region
tasks.prompt: Compare the dissent cluster to the stronger consensus evidence.

claim.title: Consensus should not erase dissent
claim.statement: A usable knowledge field keeps the main answer readable while preserving nearby disagreement and uncertainty.

answerBlocks.text: The runtime negotiates support and dissent in the same solved surface instead of exiling disagreement into hidden panels.

evidence.text: Support nodes remain near the answer when scaffold order and relation forces reinforce each other.
A plain surface makes disagreement inspectable even when field styling is muted.
Bridge overlays can expose solver narratives without owning node placement.

contradictions.text: If contradiction pressure is too weak, dissent collapses into decorative noise.
Large contradiction clusters can still become illegible if overlap pressure dominates ordering.
Teams may distrust the runtime if disagreement cannot be explained with concrete loss narratives.
